name: LA Terre
description_short: La Terre Federal Credit Union is a community-focused credit union based in Houma, Louisiana, serving over 5,100 members with traditional banking and lending services.
description_long: La Terre Federal Credit Union was established in 1970 and is headquartered in Houma, Louisiana. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative serving a specific geographic community. The institution has grown to manage over $27.5 million in assets and maintains a single branch location at 701 Barrow Street in Houma, along with one ATM. La Terre offers a comprehensive range of banking products and services including checking and savings accounts, share drafts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, and wire transfers. The credit union also provides digital banking conveniences such as online banking, a mobile app for iOS and Android, e-statements, remote deposit capture, bill pay, online loan payments, and financial calculators. Members can access drive-thru teller services and benefit from financial counseling and education resources. As a credit union, La Terre's primary distinction is its member-owned, not-for-profit structure, which typically allows it to offer competitive loan APR rates compared to traditional banks. The institution emphasizes personalized service and community focus, serving approximately 5,100 loyal members with a staff of over 12 dedicated employees. Customer reviews highlight friendly staff and competitive lending rates, though the credit union's small size limits branch access compared to larger regional or national financial institutions. La Terre is best suited for residents and local business owners in the Houma, Louisiana area seeking traditional banking services from a community-oriented institution. The main limitation is geographic—membership eligibility appears tied to the local community, and with only one physical branch, convenience is restricted to those in the Houma area. Mixed customer reviews (3.0/5 rating) suggest variable service quality experiences, indicating potential inconsistency in member interactions.
pros: Competitive APR rates on loans compared to traditional banks, per member reviews
Established 54-year history (since 1970) demonstrates stability and community roots
Comprehensive digital banking suite including mobile app, online banking, bill pay, and remote deposit capture
Personalized financial counseling and financial education services available to members
Drive-thru teller service for convenient account access without entering lobby
Member-owned, not-for-profit credit union structure typically offers better rates than commercial banks
Over 5,100 active members demonstrates established customer base and trust
cons: Only one physical branch location limits accessibility for non-local members or those who prefer in-person banking
Mixed customer reviews (3.0/5 rating) with complaints about staff professionalism and inconsistent service quality
Limited asset base ($27.5 million) compared to regional or national banks may restrict loan amounts and products
Membership eligibility restricted to specific geographic community, excluding those outside service area
No mortgage lending mentioned despite being listed as a service (content cut off mid-sentence)
best_for: Houma, Louisiana residents seeking community-focused banking with competitive lending rates
Local small business owners needing business banking services from a member-owned institution
Credit union members valuing personalized service and financial counseling over large-bank convenience
Consumers in the service area prioritizing competitive loan APRs over branch network size
diagnosis: La Terre Federal Credit Union is best for Houma-area residents and local business owners seeking community-focused banking with competitive loan rates and personalized service. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—membership is restricted to the local community, and with only one branch, accessibility is limited to those in the Houma area. Additionally, mixed customer reviews suggest inconsistent service quality experiences.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
